reasoning: The base rate of increasing bank account penetration from ~40% in 2017 to 95% by 2024 is extremely low given the infrastructure and economic constraints in Nigeria. World Bank Findex data from 2021 showed Nigeria's account ownership was likely only around 45-50%, making a jump to 95% by 2024 practically impossible. The community estimate as of October 2025 is 1%, strongly suggesting that data for 2024 has already been released or correctly anticipated to reflect a substantial miss of the target. Given the lack of time and the market consensus, the probability of reaching 95% before 2025 is as close to zero as the platform allows. I am predicting 2%.
